name: Razorpay Payment Service — Production Container Setup
scenario: Razorpay needs to containerize the payment service for consistent deployments across dev, staging, and production. Build a production-grade Docker setup: multi-stage Dockerfile, docker-compose for the full stack, and Spring Boot configuration for the container environment.

core.description: Multi-stage Dockerfile: stage 1 = eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine (Maven build + layertools extract). Stage 2 = eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine (non-root user 'razorpay', 4 COPY layers). JVM flags: MaxRAMPercentage=75.0, UseContainerSupport, urandom.

docker-compose.yml: app (build from Dockerfile) + postgres:15-alpine (pg_isready healthcheck) + redis:7-alpine (redis-cli ping healthcheck). Named pgdata volume. app depends_on postgres+redis with service_healthy.
application-docker.yml: datasource URL (postgres:5432), redis host (redis), actuator endpoints exposed, health show-details: always, Zipkin URL.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): health endpoint returns UP, /actuator/prometheus returns 200 with jvm_memory, custom counter registers. All 3 pass.
README: one docker compose up command and expected output.

bonus.description: Add zipkin + prometheus services to docker-compose.yml with correct port mappings. prometheus.yml scrape config for the app.
Docker secrets: db_password via Docker secret (not env var). application-docker.yml reads from /run/secrets/. Create scripts/setup-secrets.sh.
Container resource limits in docker-compose deploy.resources: 768m limit, 512m reservation. Calculate correct MaxRAMPercentage. Add MaxMetaspaceSize=128m.

data_to_use:
Image: eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine (builder), eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine (runtime)
Postgres: POSTGRES_DB=payments, POSTGRES_USER=payments_user, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD:-changeme}
Redis: default config, port 6379
App port: 8080
Non-root user: razorpay
ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Layertools extract: `java -Djarmode=layertools -jar target/*.jar extract` — creates 4 directories: dependencies/, spring-boot-loader/, snapshot-dependencies/, application/. COPY --from=builder: copies from the named builder stage by path. condition: service_healthy: requires a healthcheck defined on the dependency service. -XX:+UseContainerSupport: must be explicit (even though default in Java 11+ on some OSes) — good documentation practice. Spring Boot service names in docker-compose = hostname: application-docker.yml uses 'postgres' as hostname, not 'localhost'.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. Include a screenshot or terminal output showing `docker compose up` with all 4 services (app, postgres, redis) running and the app health endpoint returning UP.
